tool_call_sequence.description: Provides evidence-based first-line abortive therapy for cluster headache and demonstrates safe prescribing with required safety checks before ordering medications.

nl_assertions: Agent explains the diagnosis explicitly using the term "cluster headache" and relates it to typical features (severe unilateral orbital/temporal pain, short duration, circadian/cluster pattern, autonomic symptoms/restlessness).
Agent explains evidence-based acute treatment and must explicitly mention both "oxygen" and "sumatriptan" as first-line abortive options.
Before prescribing, agent performs an allergy check and documents/acknowledges any relevant allergies; if an allergy is present to a proposed medication, agent avoids it and offers an alternative.

Before prescribing, agent performs a contraindication check for triptans (e.g., ischemic heart disease, prior stroke/TIA, uncontrolled hypertension, hemiplegic/basilar migraine history, severe peripheral vascular disease) and avoids sumatriptan if contraindicated.

Before prescribing, agent performs a drug interaction check (notably ergot derivatives within 24 hours, other triptans, serotonergic agents with counseling about serotonin syndrome risk) and modifies the plan if significant interactions exist.

Agent screens for red flags/secondary headache features (e.g., sudden thunderclap onset, new neurologic deficits, meningismus/fever, new headache >50, immunosuppression, cancer, pregnancy/postpartum) and escalates care if present.

Agent provides clear usage instructions and limits for sumatriptan (route, timing, maximum daily dose) and oxygen (delivery method and flow rate), including expected onset of relief and what to do if ineffective.
Agent includes safety-net advice: seek urgent/emergency care for chest pain, severe shortness of breath, fainting, new neurologic symptoms, or worst headache of life.
Agent communicates in a structured, patient-centered manner, checks understanding, and avoids stigmatizing language.
